Contributing to Axon

Axon is a local-first Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework developed
by jyunming. It is published on PyPI as axon-rag and supports multiple vector
stores, embedding providers, and LLM backends.

Development Setup

Prerequisites: Python 3.10 or higher, Git, and Ollama for local LLM support.
Ollama is a runtime for running large language models locally without cloud
dependencies. The recommended development setup installs Axon in editable mode
using pip install -e ".[dev]".

Development Workflow

Tests are run with pytest. The test suite contains over 3300 tests covering
unit, integration, API, and end-to-end scenarios. The pre-commit hook runs
black, ruff, and pytest automatically before every commit.

Code formatting uses black version 23.12.1. The version is pinned in
pyproject.toml to ensure consistent formatting across all contributors and CI.
Ruff handles linting and import ordering. Mypy is used for type checking.

The pre-commit hook runs the full test suite, which takes approximately 7
minutes. Contributors should run ruff --fix before committing to avoid
hook failures on auto-fixable lint issues.

The project uses a feature branch workflow. All changes must be made on a
feature branch — never on main. Pull requests target the main branch.
The GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs on every pull request. GitHub Copilot
reviews all pull requests automatically.

PyPI publishing is tag-triggered: merging to main does not publish. A release
requires bumping the version in pyproject.toml, creating a git tag matching the
version, and pushing the tag to trigger the GitHub Actions release workflow.
